title: TensorRT
question: Why is TensorRT unsupported?
answer: TensorRT depends on NVIDIA CUDA runtime and proprietary NVIDIA GPU optimizations. It cannot execute natively on AMD GPUs. Convert models to ONNX and use ONNX Runtime with ROCm for inference.

title: torch.cuda
question: How do I migrate torch.cuda?
answer: Most PyTorch projects require only installing the ROCm-enabled PyTorch build. The torch.cuda namespace is retained in ROCm PyTorch for compatibility.

title: cudaMalloc
question: How do I replace cudaMalloc?
answer: Replace cudaMalloc with hipMalloc when migrating CUDA code to HIP. The APIs are intentionally very similar.

title: cudaMemcpy
question: How do I replace cudaMemcpy?
answer: Replace cudaMemcpy with hipMemcpy. HIP preserves almost identical semantics.

title: cudaFree
question: How do I replace cudaFree?
answer: Replace cudaFree with hipFree.

title: cudaStreamCreate
question: How do I replace CUDA streams?
answer: Replace cudaStreamCreate with hipStreamCreate.

title: cudaDeviceSynchronize
question: How do I synchronize GPU execution?
answer: Replace cudaDeviceSynchronize with hipDeviceSynchronize.

title: HIPIFY
question: What is HIPIFY?
answer: HIPIFY automatically converts much of CUDA source code into HIP-compatible code, reducing manual migration effort.

title: cuDNN
question: How do I replace cuDNN?
answer: AMD provides MIOpen as the deep learning acceleration library comparable to cuDNN.

title: cuBLAS
question: How do I replace cuBLAS?
answer: Replace cuBLAS with rocBLAS for GPU-accelerated BLAS operations.

title: cuFFT
question: How do I replace cuFFT?
answer: Use rocFFT as the AMD equivalent of cuFFT.

title: cuSPARSE
question: How do I replace cuSPARSE?
answer: AMD provides rocSPARSE for sparse matrix operations.

title: NCCL
question: How do I replace NCCL?
answer: Use RCCL for distributed GPU communication on AMD hardware.

title: CuPy
question: Does CuPy work on AMD?
answer: CuPy is CUDA-oriented. Evaluate HIP-compatible builds or migrate critical GPU code using HIP or ROCm-enabled frameworks.

title: NVIDIA Docker
question: How do I migrate Docker?
answer: Replace NVIDIA CUDA base images with ROCm images and expose AMD GPU devices such as /dev/kfd and /dev/dri.

title: AMD Developer Cloud
question: How do I deploy on AMD Developer Cloud?
answer: Provision a ROCm-enabled instance, deploy the generated ROCm container, validate GPU availability, and benchmark workloads.

title: ROCm Installation
question: How do I install ROCm?
answer: Install ROCm drivers, verify with rocm-smi, install ROCm-enabled frameworks, and validate GPU visibility.

title: Validation
question: How do I validate migration?
answer: Run functional tests, compare outputs with CUDA, benchmark inference or training, and monitor GPU utilization.

title: Benchmarking
question: How should I benchmark AMD migration?
answer: Measure latency, throughput, GPU memory usage, and compare results against the original CUDA implementation.

title: rocm-smi
question: How do I verify AMD GPU availability?
answer: Use rocm-smi to inspect GPU health, utilization, temperature, memory usage, and driver status.
